rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE ARMISTEAD CARTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Purpose Of Posting The Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] Because the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend, a further amended petition may be filed within ten (10) days after entry of this order on demurrer. Petitioner is directed to title the petition "Third Amended Petition, etc." to distinguish it from the Second Amended Petition being struck from the record by this order. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE ARMISTEAD CARTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Victoria Beuerle And Michael Paul Carters Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petitioner Eddie Caboaras Amended Petition To Set Aside The Order For Final Distribution; For Return Of Property; Conversion; Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; Constructive Fraud; Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritence; Double Damages; And Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross. C. Moody. Before the court is Respondents Victoria Beuerle and Michael Paul Carter's Demurrer to Petitioner Eddie Caboara's Amended Petition to Set Aside the Order for Final Distribution; For Return of Property; Conversion; Breach of Fiduciary Duty; Constructive Fraud; Intentional Interference with Expected Inheritance; Double Damages; and Attorney Fees. Respondents' request for judicial notice is GRANTED. Per the Notice of Demurrer, Respondents demur to all causes of action on the grounds of Petitioner's lack of standing. As pleaded in the MPA, "Petitioner makes conclusory allegations that he is the son of the Decedent, but fails to request a necessary determination from the Court, an order establishing paternity. [] Petitioner's conclusory statements regarding paternity are not sufficient to establish standing, which is a necessary element of each and every asserted cause of action." (MPA at 5:8-9, and 6:1-2). Respondents further argue the face of the judicially noticed documents rebut Petitioner's paternity claim. The Demurrer also argues Petitioner lacks standing to bring the Intentional Interference with Expected Inheritance cause of action, or to seek double damages under Probate Code sec. 850, because the Petition does not plead Respondents committed tortious acts upon Decedent as a necessary third party (intentionally interfering with the expected inheritance, or wrongfully taking or disposing of assets belonging to the estate during his lifetime, respectively). On May 12, 2025, Petitioner filed Statement of Interest for Eddie Caboara and Second Amended Petition to Set Aside the Order for Final Distribution; For Return of Property; Conversion; Breach of Fiduciary Duty; Constructive Fraud; Intentional Interference with Expected Inheritance; Double Damages; and Attorney's Fees. On May 15, 2025, Respondents filed Notice of Non-Opposition to Demurrer, arguing the Second Amended Petition was not filed properly with leave of court, and there was otherwise no opposition to the demurrer on file. On May 16, 2025, Petitioner filed Declaration in Response to Notice of Non-Opposition to Demurrer, arguing that the Second Amended Petition was filed only one day after the opposition deadline, that it repaired the omissions complained of in the Demurrer, and it was allowably filed without leave of court under Code of Civil Proc. sec. 472, in response to the Demurrer. The Declaration sought a hearing continuance to allow Respondents enough time to respond, and otherwise substantively argued against the demurrer being sustained. Respondents' Demurrer to Amended Petition is SUSTAINED, with leave to amend. The defects identified in the Demurrer are potentially correctable through an amended pleading. Petitioner acknowledged this fact, and attempted to cure perceived defects through the filing of a second amended petition. He asserts that he was entitled to do so under Code of Civil Procedure section 472. He is mistaken: because he had already filed an amended petition, he needed leave of court to file a second amended petition. Accordingly, Second Amended Petition to Set Aside the Order for Final Distribution; For Return of Property; Conversion; Breach of Fiduciary Duty; Constructive Fraud; Intentional Interference with Expected Inheritance; Double Damages; and Attorney's Fees filed May 12, 2025 is ORDERED STRUCK FROM THE RECORD (see, Hodges v. Cnty. of Placer (2019) 41 Cal. App. 5th 537, 544).
